title: antirrhinum molle subsp. lopesianum
intro: Antirrhinum molle subsp. lopesianum, also known as the Antirrhinum lopesianum, is a subspecies of the Antirrhinum molle plant, which belongs to the snapdragon family (Scrophulariaceae) and is native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Antirrhinum molle subsp. lopesianum include its ability to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, as well as its potential to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
